faqs.question: How much does lawn mowing cost in Birmingham, AL?
faqs.answer: Lawn mowing in Birmingham costs $52 per visit on average, with most homeowners paying $42–$67 for a standard residential lawn. Monthly plans typically run $170–$230. Birmingham's warm climate means a long growing season — typically 7-8 active months with Bermuda and Zoysia grass dominant. Spring prep and fall cleanup are peak seasons. Pricing varies by lot size — small lots (under 5,000 sq ft) run lower; large properties (10,000+ sq ft) run higher.

faqs.question: How much does tree-service cost in Birmingham?
faqs.answer: Tree Service projects in Birmingham vary widely by scope. Basic cleanup and mulch runs $390–$780. A mid-size front yard redesign costs $1,800–$5,500. Full backyard transformation with hardscape, plants, and irrigation runs $5,500–$12,000. Always get 3 quotes and a written scope before starting.

faqs.question: How much does sprinkler/irrigation installation cost in Birmingham?
faqs.answer: Sprinkler system installation in Birmingham runs $2,000–$5,500, averaging $3,200 for a standard residential lot. Smart controllers add $150–$400 but can reduce water bills 20–40%. Birmingham's warm climate means a long growing season — typically 7-8 active months with Bermuda and Zoysia grass dominant. Spring prep and fall cleanup are peak seasons.

faqs.question: How much does tree trimming cost in Birmingham?
faqs.answer: Tree trimming in Birmingham costs $210–$310 for small trees (under 30 ft) and $620–$920 for large trees. Emergency tree removal after storms runs higher — $500–$2,500 depending on size and accessibility. Always verify tree service companies carry adequate liability insurance before hiring.

faqs.question: How often should I have my lawn mowed in Birmingham?
faqs.answer: In Birmingham, march–october (peak: april–june) is the active growing season. During peak growth (spring/summer), weekly mowing keeps lawns healthy and prevents scalping. Bi-weekly works during slower growth periods. Birmingham's warm climate means a long growing season — typically 7-8 active months with Bermuda and Zoysia grass dominant. Spring prep and fall cleanup are peak seasons. Most Birmingham tree-service companies offer flexible weekly, bi-weekly, or monthly plans.

faqs.question: What's the best time to do tree-service in Birmingham?
faqs.answer: The best time for major tree-service projects in Birmingham is early spring (March–April) or early fall (September–October) when temperatures are moderate and plants establish well. Birmingham's warm climate means a long growing season — typically 7-8 active months with Bermuda and Zoysia grass dominant. Spring prep and fall cleanup are peak seasons. Booking early in spring is critical — most reputable Birmingham landscapers are booked 3–6 weeks out by May.

faqs.question: How do I choose a tree-service company in Birmingham?
faqs.answer: Look for Birmingham landscapers who: (1) are licensed and carry general liability + workers' comp, (2) have 4.5+ star ratings with 50+ reviews, (3) provide written quotes with a defined scope of work, (4) have been operating locally for 5+ years, (5) offer a satisfaction guarantee. Avoid cash-only contractors or those who can't provide a certificate of insurance.

faqs.question: Do tree-service companies in Birmingham offer seasonal contracts?
faqs.answer: Yes — most Birmingham tree-service companies offer seasonal or annual maintenance contracts that bundle mowing, edging, fertilizing, mulching, and cleanup. Annual contracts typically save 10–20% vs. per-service pricing and guarantee priority scheduling. Ask about start-of-season aeration and overseeding packages, which are popular in Birmingham's climate.

buyerTips: Always get 3 written quotes before hiring a landscaper in Birmingham — prices for the same job vary 25–40%.
Verify the company carries general liability insurance ($1M minimum) and workers' comp — accidents on your property without coverage can become your liability.
Ask for references from Birmingham homeowners who had similar work done — a reputable landscaper will provide them without hesitation.
Get everything in writing: scope of work, materials, plant species/varieties, timeline, and payment schedule.
For new plantings, ask about the warranty — good Birmingham landscapers guarantee plants for 1 growing season.
Book spring projects early (January–February) — the best Birmingham landscapers fill their calendars by March.
Annual maintenance contracts save 10–20% over one-off services and guarantee consistent quality throughout the season.
